title: Theory of Computation - Identify Class Language

questions.question:
Given the following two languages:

  • \( L_1=\{a^nba^n\;|\;n>0\} \)
  • \( L_2=\{a^nba^nb^{n+1}\;|\;n>0\} \)

Which of the following is correct?



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | December 2015 | Part 3 | Question: 28
options: \( L_1 \) is context free language and \( L_2 \) is not context free language
\( L_1 \) is not context free language and \( L_2 \) is context free language
Both \( L_1 \) and \( L_2 \) are context free languages
Both \( L_1 \) and \( L_2 \) are not context free languages

questions.question:
Consider the following languages:

\( L_1 = \{ a^nb^nc^m \} \cup \{a^nb^mc^m\}, n, m \geq 0 \)

\( L_2 =\{ww^R \mid w \in\{ a, b \}^*\} \) Where \( R \) represents reversible operation.

Which one of the following is (are) inherently ambiguous languages(s)?



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | December 2019 | Part 2 | Question: 48

Given the following two statements:

  1. \( L=\{w\mid n_{a}(w)=n_{b}(w)\} \) is deterministic context free language, but not linear.
  2. \( L=\{a^{n}b^{n}\} \cup \{a^{n}b^{2n} \} \) is linear, but not deterministic context free language.

Which of the following options is correct?



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | January 2017 | Part 3 | Question: 61

Given the following two languages : 

\( L_{1}=\left\{a^{n}b^{n}|n\geq 1\right\} \cup \left\{a\right\} \)

\( L_{2}=\left\{w C w^{R}|w \in \left\{a, b\right\}^{*}\right\} \)

Which statement is correct ?



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | June 2014 | Part 3 | Question: 75
options: Both \( L_{1} \) and \( L_{2} \) are not deterministic.
\( L_{1} \) is not deterministic and \( L_{2} \) is deterministic.
\( L_{1} \) is deterministic and \( L_{2} \) is not deterministic.

questions.question:
Match \( \text{List I} \) with \( \text{List II} \)

\( L_R: \) Regular language, \( LCF \): Context free language

\( L_{REC}: \) Recursive langauge, \( L_{RE} \): Recursively enumerable language.

\( \begin{array}{llll} & \text{List I} & & \text{List II} \\ (A) & \text{Recursively Enumerable language} & (I) & \overline{L}_{REC} \cup L_{RE} \\ (B) & \text{Recursive language} & (II) & \overline{L}_{CF} \cup L_{REC} \\ (C) & \text{Context Free language} & (III) & L_R^* \cap L_{CF} \end{array} \)

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | October 2020 | Part 2 | Question: 71

The language accepted by the non-deterministic pushdown automaton

\( M=( \{ q_0, q_1, q_2\}, \{a, b\}, \{ a, b, z\}, \delta, q_0, z, \{q_2\}) \) with transitions

\( \delta (q_0 a, z) = \{ (q_1 a), (q_2 \lambda) \} \);

\( \delta (q_1, b, a) = \{ (q_1, b) \} \)

\( \delta (q_1, b, b) = \{ (q_1 b) \}, \delta (q_1, a, b) = \{ (q_2, \lambda ) \} \) is



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | September 2013 | Part 3 | Question: 19

The language \( L=\{a^n b^n a^m b^m \mid n \geq 0, m \geq 0 \} \) is



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | September 2013 | Part 3 | Question: 20

Assume statements \( S_1 \) and \( S_2 \) defined as:

\( S_1 \): \( L_2 – L_1 \) is recursive enumerable where \( L_1 \) and \( L_2 \) are recursive and recursive enumerable respectively.

\( S_2 \): The set of all Turing machines is countable.

Which of the following is true?



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | September 2013 | Part 3 | Question: 21
options: \( S_1 \) is correct and \( S_2 \) is not correct
Both \( S_1 \) and \( S_2 \) are not correct
\( S_1 \) is not correct and \( S_2 \) is correct
